text: 1 IDTA - Digital Twin Partner Summit “North America” in Boston 4/5th June 2025 Prof. Dr. Dieter Wegener | VP Siemens AG & ZVEI-Speaker „Industrie 4.0“ Digital Product Passport 4.0 DPP4.0 – an innovative concept for implementing the DPP as the Digital Twin of a Product IDTA - Digital Twin Partner Summit “North America”| 4/5th June 2025 | Prof. Dr. Dieter Wegener 4IDTA - Digital Twin Partner Summit “North America”| 4/5th June 2025 | Prof. Dr. Dieter Wegener EU Digital Product Passport (DPP) 30th March 2022: EC publishes proposal for a Ecodesign for Sustainable Product Regulation (ESPR) COM(2022) 142 final 18th July 2024: entered into force ▪ Requirements for DPP ▪ Standardization Request on DPP announced ▪ Digital product passport to be rolled out across sectors by 2030 ESPR has been published in EU‘s Official Journal 5IDTA - Digital Twin Partner Summit “North America”| 4/5th June 2025 | Prof. Dr. Dieter Wegener 2024 ESPR* and CSRD* Publication of the first version of the Cross-Sectoral and Sectoral DPP Roadmaps (CIRPASS) Start of the implementation of pilot projects in various industries (DPP4.0, ZVEI ENGEL Showcase) 2026 Expanding DPP requirements to include additional product categories, including construction products and consumer products Implementation of training and information campaigns for companies 2028-2029 Extension of DPP requirements to other product categories (steel or aluminium) Further development of the digital infrastructure for DPP implementation 2025 Introduction of the first mandatory requirements for certain product categories, such as textiles and electronics Further development of technical standards and interoperability guidelines More regulation on the Digital Product Passport (DPP) will follow 2027 Entry into force of digital documentation Machine Directive and the Battery Passport DPP Regulation as of February 2027 Putting in place monitoring and enforcement mechanisms to ensure compliance 2030 Full integration of the DPP into the European circular economy Assessment of the impact and achievements of the DPP initiative and planning further steps for optimization *ESPR Ecodesign Sustainability Product Regulation *CSRD Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive 6IDTA - Data based on ECLASS Advanced (delivery documentation) AAS Control Cabinet DNP 4.0 PCF* Handover Documentation (e.g. RoHS, REACH) CAx-Container (customized) BOMLevel 1New forLevel 2 * PCF = Product Carbon Footprint, equals CO2-Footprint, Cradle-to-Gate-Calculation 34IDTA - Digital Twin Partner Summit “North America”| 4/5th June 2025 | Prof. Dr. Dieter Wegener HMI-2024 and SPS-2024: DPP4.0-Example for „Digital Service“ Use-Case – Energy Monitoring Control Cabinet Mandatory (Regulation) Other Digital Services DNP 4.0 Public Submodel Restricted Submodel Accessing individual AAS via ID-Link on each device to aggregate PCF of the cabinet Monitoring and documenting live energy consumption of the cabinet via energy monitoring device 35IDTA - Digital Twin Partner Summit “North America”| 4/5th June 2025 | Prof. Dr. Dieter Wegener EU enforces DPP and DPP-System1 Industry-Approach DPP4.02 Industry-Approach DPP4.0-System3 DPP4.0 - UseCase PMD* 4 *PMD = Paperless Machine Documentation 5 DPP4.0 – ShowCase „CO2@Control Cabinet“ 36IDTA - Digital Twin Partner Summit “North America”| 4/5th June 2025 | Prof. Dr. Dieter Wegener Page 36 Restricted | © Siemens 2024 | Marzo | DI FA ABP BAS LCY | 2024-08-06 The machine documentation can be provided in a digital format Digital provision of… • Instructions of use • EU declaration of conformity • Instruction for installation • Assembly instructions for partly completed machinery • Documents for assembly, installation and connection of machines § Guide for the application of the Machinery Directive 2006/42/EC – Version 2.3 (04/2024): Several documents may now be provided by the manufacturer in a digital format. The path towards a paperless machine documentation is enabled. Machinery Regulation (EU) 2023/1230 valid from 20/01/2027: “Manufacturers shall ensure that the machinery or related products are accompanied by the instructions for use and the information set out in Annex III. The instructions may be provided in a digital format.” 37IDTA - Digital Twin Partner Summit “North America”| 4/5th June 2025 | Prof. Dr. Dieter Wegener Page 37 DPP4.0 enables paperless and sustainable machine documentation via the Asset Administration Shell (AAS) Status Quo Solution 15 kg of paper ~ 10-18 kg of CO2e Digitally available machine documentationDigital Nameplate(accordingIEC61406-1/-2)Handover Documentation Asset Administration Shell (according IEC63278)Hierarchical Structures enabling Bills of Material IDTA standardized Sub-models 38IDTA - Digital Twin Partner Summit “North America”| 4/5th June 2025 | Prof. Dr. Dieter Wegener Page 38 The software also provides the documentation of the components 15 kg of paper ~ 15 kg of CO2 SENTRON 7KM PAC4200 Measurement device Pressure control valve DBDS 20 K1X/100P050 Poppet directional valve M-3SED6CK1X / 350CG24N9K4/V SITOP UPS1600 10 A Power Supply 39IDTA - Digital Twin Partner Summit “North America”| 4/5th June 2025 | Prof. Dr. Dieter Wegener Page 39 DPP4.0 enables paperless and sustainable machine documentation via the Asset Administration Shell (AAS) Digitally available machine documentation 40IDTA - Digital Twin Partner Summit “North America”| 4/5th June 2025 | Prof. Dr. Dieter Wegener Authentication Service DPP-Viewer Restricted | © Siemens 2024 | Laporta | DI FA ABP BAS LCY | 2024-08-06Page 40 Economic Operator: Employee User/Customer AAS Connector: X-Integrator Request Document via Machine DIDAuthenticate via Authentication Service SaaS Data input (e.g. via e-mail) AAS Server Database Supplier: • . • . • ... Any questions? 42IDTA - Digital Twin Partner Summit “North America”| 4/5th June 2025 | Prof. Dr. Dieter Wegener Contact Information Prof. Dr. Dieter Wegener Head of External Cooperation, Siemens Foundational Technology Otto-Hahn-Ring 6, 81739 Munich Mobile: +49 (173) 2512980, E-mail: dieter.wegener@siemens.com Other external activities: (1) since 2014 Chair of ZVEI Management Circle “Industrie 4.0”, Frankfurt (ZVEI = Electro and Digital Industry Association) (2) since 2015 Vice-President DKE, Frankfurt (DKE = German Commission for Electrical, Electronic & Information Technologies of DIN and VDE) (3) since 2016 Chair of Advisory Board SCI4.0 (Co-Founder), Frankfurt (SCI4.0 = “Standardization Council Industrie 4.0”) (4) since 2019 Vice-Chair of DMEC (Co-Founder), Digital Europe, Brussels (DMEC = Digital Manufacturing Executive Council) (5) since 2019 Chair of DIN/DKE Strategic Advisory Board FOCUS.digital, DIN/Berlin and DKE/Frankfurt (former DIN Presidential Committee FOCUS.ICT for “German ICT- Standardization”, DIN, Berlin) (6) since 2020 Honorary Professor, RWTH Aachen University, TIME-Institute (7) since 2023 Member of “German Strategy Forum for Standardization at BMWK”, Berlin (BMWK = Federal Ministry for Economics and Climate Action) (8) since 2023 Chair of BDI-Taskforce DPP, Berlin (BDI = Federation of German Industries) (9) since 2024 Chair of ZVEI-Forum DPP4.0, Frankfurt
